name: NotebookLM
one_liner: Google's AI research-and-writing assistant that grounds summaries, Q&A, and audio overviews in your uploaded documents; the NotebookLM Enterprise edition on Google Cloud exposes a REST API for programmatic notebook and source management.
access_notes: The only official/documented API is NotebookLM Enterprise, part of Gemini Enterprise on Google Cloud. It requires a Google Cloud project with NotebookLM Enterprise licensing enabled, enterprise identity (Cloud Identity / Entra / Okta - not personal Google accounts), and the Cloud NotebookLM User/Admin IAM roles. A GCP 'Start free' trial exists but the enterprise product itself must be provisioned/licensed, so working API access is effectively gated behind a paid enterprise plan and admin setup. The consumer NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com) has NO public API.
api_notes: REST API under the discoveryengine.googleapis.com/v1alpha surface (regional endpoints us-/eu-/global-). Documented methods are a handful: notebooks.create, notebooks.get, notebooks.listRecentlyViewed (last 500), notebooks.batchDelete, notebooks.share, and sources:batchCreate for adding data sources. Auth via bearer token (gcloud auth print-access-token / service-account OAuth). No documented chat/query or audio-overview generation endpoints in the public API yet.
mcp_notes: No Google/vendor-shipped MCP server. Several community projects exist: PleasePrompto/notebooklm-mcp (drives a real Chrome via Patchright against consumer NotebookLM) and jacob-bd/notebooklm-mcp-cli (CLI + MCP + agent skills). These are unofficial and mostly automate the consumer web UI rather than the Enterprise REST API.
main_blocker: A real public REST API exists but only in the Enterprise edition, which requires a licensed Google Cloud / Gemini Enterprise setup plus IAM roles - not self-serve. Consumer NotebookLM has no official API.

evidence.claim: NotebookLM Enterprise documents REST methods notebooks.create/get/listRecentlyViewed/batchDelete/share with bearer-token (gcloud access token) auth on the discoveryengine v1alpha endpoint.
sources:batchCreate endpoint adds/manages data sources in a notebook via the API.
NotebookLM Enterprise requires a Google Cloud project, enterprise identity, and Cloud NotebookLM Admin/User IAM roles; sold standalone or as part of Gemini Enterprise.
Community MCP server for NotebookLM (PleasePrompto/notebooklm-mcp) drives Chrome to give agents grounded citation-backed answers - not an official Google MCP.

open_questions: Exact licensing cost and whether the GCP free trial actually provisions a working NotebookLM Enterprise API key without sales contact; whether query/chat and audio-overview generation will be added to the public REST API.
